summary: Reviewed shard C's 16 technical, follow-up, ambiguity, and unsupported-boundary cases. Recommendation: keep all 16 in shard C, with explicit execution setup separating seeded selected-project turns, ordinary RAG ambiguity checks, corpus-wide technical discovery, and unsupported/current-or-missing-field refusals.

inputs.purpose: Primary 16-case shard C input under review.
Neighbor shard distribution check only; no edits.
Neighbor shard distribution check only; no edits.
Neighbor shard distribution check only; no edits.
Confirmed proposal-only worker policy and shard task description.

accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: The shard is coherent as a boundary-heavy technical/follow-up lane: 5 technical requirement extraction cases, 3 ambiguity or correction cases, 6 conversation follow-up cases, and 2 unsupported/current-or-missing-field refusal cases. No case needs removal before execution.

accepted_fields_or_changes.execution_setup: Run with an explicit selected project seed before the user question. The seed should name one target project/document set and should be retained as the answer target across retrieval and generation.
accepted_fields_or_changes.scoring_note: Score as selected-project technical extraction, not corpus-wide discovery. A correct answer may say the seeded project has no visible requirement for a requested field if that absence is grounded.

accepted_fields_or_changes.execution_setup: Run as corpus-wide technical discovery even though the input metadata currently marks seed_required=true. The seed should be a scope instruction such as '전체 데이터 기준' rather than a selected project, or the scorer should explicitly ignore selected-project retention for these two cases.
accepted_fields_or_changes.scoring_note: These should not be penalized for returning multiple projects. They should be penalized if AI/compliance keywords are inferred from titles only without body evidence, or if security/legal compliance is overgeneralized from boilerplate.

accepted_fields_or_changes.execution_setup: Run as ordinary RAG text with no conversation seed. Retrieval should preserve enough near-duplicate/title/org evidence to let the answer correct the user's partial title, duplicate candidate, or wrong organization premise.
accepted_fields_or_changes.scoring_note: Credit should favor bounded correction over forced matching. Q062 must handle partial-title plus AI-prediction absence; Q063 must compare near duplicates without merging them; Q064 must correct the wrong organization name rather than silently accepting it.

accepted_fields_or_changes.execution_setup: Run only after a concrete prior turn has established the referent project or comparison set. The seed must include the previous answer or a compact conversation state with selected project ids, not merely a free-text title hint.
accepted_fields_or_changes.scoring_note: These are not valid as standalone questions. Q069 also needs numeric budget extraction and ratio calculation; Q072 and Q073 should test style transformation and term explanation without adding new unsupported project facts.

accepted_fields_or_changes.execution_setup: Run as boundary/refusal cases. Q075 should be seeded only with the available corpus date/document state and must not browse or imply live bid availability unless the eval harness explicitly includes a current procurement source. Q076 should be seeded with a notice where amount is absent and should refuse exact-budget estimation while offering concrete next checks.
accepted_fields_or_changes.scoring_note: Reward useful bounded refusal, not blank refusal. The answer may explain that provided documents cannot prove current availability or exact budget, and may suggest official procurement-status or issuer-budget checks.

accepted_fields_or_changes.reason: The shard title is accurate if the merger treats it as a mixed execution shard. Rename is unnecessary; the key requirement is that lane-specific scoring prevents technical discovery, follow-up memory, and unsupported refusal cases from being averaged as one ordinary RAG score.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Moving Q075 or Q076 to shard D
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: D already contains unsupported/legal/business guarantee cases Q077, Q079-Q081. Keeping Q075-Q076 here gives shard C its boundary tail and tests whether follow-up/technical prompts degrade into unsupported current-status or estimation claims.

B already covers selected-project contract and technical extract cases Q046-Q050. Moving all five would overconcentrate technical review gates in B and leave C as mostly follow-up/refusal. Keep them, but split Q054-Q055 execution from selected-project seeded cases.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Executing Q067-Q073 without a prior conversational seed
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The questions contain deictic references such as '그 사업', '둘 중', '그와 비슷한', '위 내용', and '방금 말한'. Standalone execution would create artificial failures unrelated to RAG quality.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Treating Q054-Q055 as selected-project follow-ups because seed_required=true
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: Their question text and target_docs indicate whole-corpus discovery. A selected-project seed would bias retrieval and undercount valid cross-project matches.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Scoring Q075 with real-time web truth
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The eval corpus cannot establish current bid availability. Unless the harness includes an explicit live source, the expected behavior is refusal to guarantee current status plus next-check guidance.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Scoring Q076 as an arithmetic estimation task
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: The task asks for an exact budget where the amount is absent. The correct boundary is to refuse exact inference and avoid filling procurement blanks from similar projects or averages.

rejected_or_risky_items.item: Single aggregate shard pass rate without sidecar issue counts
rejected_or_risky_items.risk: This shard intentionally mixes answerable extraction, ambiguity correction, follow-up memory, calculation, style transformation, and refusal. Aggregate scoring without sidecars will hide over-refusal, project mixing, context loss, and unsupported-claim failures.

needs_orchestrator_review: Confirm whether the final execution payload can override Q054-Q055 seed semantics from selected-project seed to corpus-wide scope seed without editing protected shard files.
Before answer execution, define concrete seed contexts for Q051-Q053, Q067-Q073, Q075, and Q076; record the seed ids in sidecar output so failures are attributable.
For Q069, require numeric sidecar fields for both budgets, ratio, and missing-budget caveat if either budget is absent.
For Q072 and Q073, make sure the scorer distinguishes style/term-explanation quality from unsupported new factual claims.

case_level_reviews.risk: May hallucinate testing/performance/stability requirements if the seeded project only has generic quality clauses.
Operations, deployment, monitoring, and disaster-recovery terms can be boilerplate; require evidence spans for each returned requirement.
Data migration may be absent or phrased indirectly; score missing-field caveat rather than forcing a positive answer.
High overfit risk to buzzwords AI, prediction, analysis, automation; require evidence that a returned project actually demands the feature.
Compliance terms are common boilerplate; scorer should require the specific requested class such as web accessibility, privacy, security review, or legal compliance.
Partial title plus AI-prediction query can invite false positive keyword matching; reward explicit absence handling.
Near-duplicate titles can be merged accidentally; require separate issuer/title/date or scope evidence for each candidate.
Wrong organization name should be corrected, not copied into the answer as fact.
Context target loss if the answer retrieves generic maintenance requirements from a different project.
Budget ratio can be wrong if units or VAT/included estimates differ; require calculation trace.
Similarity expansion can overfit to education keywords and miss agency diversity; require why each result is similar.
External integration and migration details are easy to fabricate from common RFP patterns; require supported bullets or explicit absence.
Primarily tests context compression and executive summary style; should not be scored as new retrieval recall.
May blend general knowledge with document context; acceptable only if it clearly separates general explanation from document-grounded claims.
High risk of unsupported live-status claim or excessive refusal. Correct answer should state corpus limitation and suggest official current-status checks.
High risk of filling missing exact budget from averages or neighboring projects. Correct answer should refuse exact estimation and identify acceptable evidence sources.

commands_run.command: Python json load and print id/segment/intent/question for shard C
Python counters for lane, family, answerability_source, seed_required, and sidecar_blockers
Read A/B/D shard JSON summaries for neighbor-shard distribution only
Searched eval for sidecar blocker names and prior parallel_team_worker_output.v1 examples

observed_results: Sidecar blockers present: context_target_retention/missing_field_caveat/seed_project_required for 11 cases; body_visible_support/missing_required_claim/project_mixing for 3 cases; over_refusal/unsupported_guarantee_refusal/useful_next_checks for 2 cases.
No protected source, script, eval final shard, experiment log, task list, or work journal files were edited.
The only intended write is this proposal contract JSON at the allowed output path.
